summary: Iteration 15 backend + frontend tested. Backend: 18/18 pytest passing — WebSocket (connect w/ valid JWT, ping→pong, bad-token 4401 close, missing-token validation error, tenant isolation via message_event), RBAC v2 manager role (SA can create manager platform-user, CA can create manager scoped to company_id, CA blocked from creating admin/super_admin with correct error text, agent back-compat, hierarchy — manager can GET /contacts but 403 on DELETE /contacts), Email plumbing (config unconfigured for SA, 403 for CA, 503 on /test when key absent), Campaign control (pause running=200, pause completed=400, cancel running=200), Regression (single /api/messages/send still works, /api/wallets SA list). Frontend: SA login → /dashboard, /api/ws WSS connection auto-opens and receives {type:connected, role:super_admin, server_time}. Team page → Add Member dialog → member-role-select dropdown shows all 4 roles for SA (agent, manager, admin, super_admin).

issues: React warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}' — a11y warning from Radix Dialog. Add DialogDescription.

Recharts console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' — chart containers rendering at 0px before layout stabilises. Set minWidth/minHeight on ResponsiveContainer parent.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix Radix DialogContent a11y — add DialogDescription to /team Add Member dialog (and audit others).
OPTIONAL: Give Recharts ResponsiveContainer parents an explicit min-height to silence width/height=-1 warnings on Dashboard.
Consider migrating in-memory WSHub to Redis pub/sub for horizontal scaling (already noted in code comment).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has grown to 3012 lines — extract WS hub, email helpers, campaign runner, and wallet code into /app/backend/routers/*.py modules (previously flagged, still open).
adjust_wallet still uses two-step increment-then-rollback for negative-balance guard — racy under concurrent adjusts. Prefer aggregation-pipeline update with $expr guard (flagged iter 14).
run_campaign polls campaign status per-recipient via find_one — for large audiences this is O(N) DB reads. Consider a cached status + periodic refresh (e.g. every 10 recipients or every 5s).

WebSocket /api/ws token comes from query string — logged into supervisor access logs. Prefer sending token via first WS text frame after accept, or via subprotocol header, to avoid token leakage in log rotation.
useRealtime.js schedules reconnect even when the WS closes cleanly due to logout — check ws.close(1000, ...) code and skip reconnect on 1000/4401. Currently backoff still fires (harmless but noisy).
email_service (Resend/SendGrid) is env-gated. Verified 503 path works. When adding keys, verify the campaign-complete email hook actually runs (currently untested with real key).
test_credentials: SA admin@cpaas.io / Admin@12345. Ephemeral tenants TEST_IT15_A_<ts>, TEST_IT15_B_<ts> created and deleted per module.
seed_data_creation: Two ephemeral tenants per test module (A + B) for tenant-isolation and RBAC checks — auto-cleaned via yield teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 15 fully verified. Backend WS at /api/ws?token=<JWT> — closes with code 4401 on bad token, accepts+sends {type:connected} on valid, echoes pong for 'ping' text frames, keepalive every 45s. WSHub is in-memory keyed by company_id (or '__platform__' for SA). Tenant isolation confirmed via /api/messages/send emit_event path — SMS send by tenant A does not leak to tenant B's WS. RBAC v2 hierarchy in place: agent=1, manager=2, admin=3, super_admin=4; require_roles uses min-level check so manager satisfies agent-only endpoints. CA cannot create admin/super_admin (403 'Company admin can only create Manager or Agent users'). Email service env-gated — /api/email/config returns configured:false when RESEND_API_KEY/SENDGRID_API_KEY absent, /api/email/test returns 503. Campaign pause/resume/cancel wired to require_roles('super_admin','admin','manager') for pause+resume; cancel is admin+SA only. Note: tenant-webhook inbound_message test was replaced with message_event test because /api/webhook/whatsapp/{cid} requires a pre-existing company_whatsapp_configs doc + valid X-Hub signature — infeasible to simulate without real WA setup. If future iterations add a test-mode webhook bypass, wire it up.
rca of the issue: No new bugs found. Two minor initial test-authoring issues self-fixed: (1) REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL not exported into pytest env — read from /app/frontend/.env fallback. (2) POST /api/messages/send returns 'message_id' key not 'id' — assertion relaxed. Tenant-webhook route requires WA config + Meta signature; test rewritten to use /api/messages/send which triggers the same WS broadcast path (emit_event → ws_hub.broadcast).
